subject: math
source_title: New General Mathematics SSS 1 by M.F. Macrae et al
learning_objectives: Identify and describe properties of cube, cuboid, cylinder, triangular prism, cone and rectangular-based pyramid
Calculate the volume of prisms (cube, cuboid, cylinder, triangular prism)
Calculate the curved surface area and total surface area of a cylinder
Calculate the curved surface area and total surface area of a cone
Calculate the volume of a cone and pyramids

sections.heading: Properties of Solid Shapes
sections.content: A cube has 12 edges, 8 vertices and 6 square faces. A cuboid has 12 edges, 8 vertices and 6 rectangular faces. A triangular prism has 9 edges, 6 vertices, 3 rectangular faces and 2 triangular end faces. A cylinder has 2 circular faces, 1 curved surface and 2 curved edges. A cone has 1 vertex, 2 curved edges, 1 curved surface and 1 circular base. A rectangular-based pyramid has 8 edges, 5 vertices, 4 triangular faces and 1 rectangular base.

sections.heading: Volume and Surface Area of Prisms
sections.content: A prism has uniform cross section. Volume of prism = area of cross section × height. Specific formulas: Cube: V = l³, SA = 6l². Cuboid: V = lbh, SA = 2(lb + lh + bh). Cylinder: V = πr²h; curved surface area = 2πrh; total surface area = 2πr(h + r). Example: cylinder with r = 7cm, h = 4cm → V = (22/7) × 49 × 4 = 616cm³; total SA = 2 × (22/7) × 7 × (4 + 7) = 484cm².

sections.heading: Surface Area of a Cone
sections.content: The curved surface of a cone is formed by a sector of a circle. The relationship between the sector angle θ, slant height l and base radius r is θ/360 = r/l. Curved surface area of cone = πrl. Total surface area = πrl + πr² = πr(l + r). Example: cone with diameter 8cm (r = 4cm) and height 3cm → slant height l = √(3² + 4²) = 5cm; curved SA = π × 4 × 5 = 20π cm².

sections.heading: Volume of Pyramids and Cones
sections.content: Volume = (1/3) × base area × height. Specific formulas: square-based pyramid: V = (1/3)b²h; rectangular-based pyramid: V = (1/3)lbh; cone: V = (1/3)πr²h. Examples: pyramid 8cm high on a 6cm × 4cm base → V = (1/3) × 6 × 4 × 8 = 64cm³. Cone with diameter 14cm (r=7cm) and height 24cm → V = (1/3) × (22/7) × 49 × 24 = 1232cm³.
